derivation: Panel: worldgen 37, player 38, rendering 37, systems 40, perf 38. Median = 38. Systems' 40 is the unverified-heavy outlier: it credits ~5 of 7 passive+hostile mob weights while natural spawn ticks, despawn, and sun-burn were explicitly unobservable in this round's sub-second frame pumps, and it banks ~1 structure weight on micro-ruins; discounting it pulls the merge below the raw median. The two 37s are not symmetric drags — worldgen's deficit is mostly verification debt on already-landed code (de-jitter, mountains, snow line node-verified but never play-converted), and rendering withholds on structurally-unhearable audio and two probe-starved cores. Harsh, ties-down: 37.5. Delta +2.5 vs round 3's 35, decomposed: weight-4 crafting family flipped from probe-proven fail to live-verified end-to-end (+~1.3), mob layer live across render/AI/combat/drops/death-respawn but discounted for the unverified ecology legs and the verified player-knockback fail (+~1.5 net), first structures-family credit at micro scale (+~0.5), input-core conversions (look, wheel, dusk/night, cloud drift) (+~0.3), minus expectation pressure from five grep-proven-absent families (light, weather, smelting, farming, redstone) and five perf foundations carried unaddressed a third round (-~0.6).

probe.observations: ENVIRONMENT: this round's frame pumps were far smaller than round 3's (~0.05-0.15s of sim per screenshot vs ~0.5s; gt crept ~1 game-minute per pump). Consequence: anything needing >1s of continuous sim (natural mob-spawn ticks, zombie 1.5s sun-burn, the 0.8s drop-pickup immunity window) was hard to observe live — I verified the porkchop drop spawned, rested on ground, and magnetized toward the player, but never saw it cross into inventory before budget pressure moved me on (round 3 verified drop auto-collect for apples; addItem itself proven via give()). Natural spawn/despawn and zombie daylight burning are therefore UNVERIFIED live (code paths read clean: night-only zombie waves <=4 within r18-32, day pigs on grass columns, despawn at pd>56, sunT>=1.5 burn with particle burst) - do not award or dock them on my account. FPS was unmeasurable (HUD reads 0 while hidden; 60 on fresh visible load; no sign of regression even with 8 zombies + particle bursts). HUD text (XYZ/biome/clock) is throttled/one-frame stale - screenshots sometimes show the previous camera's HUD line. Zero console errors and zero __vox.errors across the whole session including two loads, a death, 8 simultaneous mobs, and the full crafting choreography. GAME FEEL: the mob layer makes the world feel inhabited - pigs amble between trees, flee when struck (with a proper red hurt-flash), die into pink particle bursts and a spinning textured porkchop; zombies path straight at you at night and the surrounding-pack death was genuinely menacing. The round-3 headline bug is FIXED beautifully: recipes now match >= counts with exact-amount consumption and leftovers, right-click half-stack splitting exists, sticks even got a hotbar icon + label. The crafting screenshot (grid leftovers 1 plank/4 planks/2 sticks, output offering a second pick, crafted pick showing a green durability bar) is the money shot. Structures are charming: broken mossy-stone ruin rings with a crafting table centerpiece on flowered plains, and desert wells (stone rim, corner posts, 1-deep water) among cacti. The desert floating islets at (560,115) are gone - 0 non-foliage floating blocks in a 56x61-block scan (the only 'floaters' are tree canopies). One design note for judges: zombie hits apply NO knockback impulse to the player (damage flash + hp only) - filed as a fail since the round prompt asked explicitly, but it reads as a scope choice, not a bug; mobs themselves DO take knockback (vx/vz=6, vy=4 on hit, observed as flee+stun). Pig portrait, hurt-flash, death burst+drop, night zombie, dusk sky, ruin, well, and crafting UI all captured in screenshots. Items I deliberately did not re-verify (round-3 passes, code unchanged): pause-freeze, AO corners, tool speed timing, hunger drain mechanics, underwater effect, particles.

panel.summary: Round 4 finally moved my domain after two stagnant rounds: the floating-islet regression is verifiably dead (0 non-foliage floaters in a 56x61 scan where round 3 found two), vegetation rules flips back to pass, and the villages/structures family banks its first credit ever — wells and ruin rings verified block-for-block at two sites with screenshots, deterministically seeded and layered under player edits. The de-jitter, beach clamp, and mountain boost also landed and were node-execution-verified, but the probe never play-converted them, so coherent regions, biome shapes, and the snow line stay uncredited alongside the perennially unobserved rivers/tint/iron-depth — roughly 1.5 MPS of pure verification debt sitting in my domain. Build-wide through my lens, the delta is +2 over the round-3 panel's 35: the crafting reachability fix and the live-verified pig/zombie layer complete the 50-band ingredient list (inventory, crafting, health, basic mobs all now play-proven), worth roughly +8 systems family weights, tempered by the player-knockback fail, the unverified natural-spawn/sun-burn legs, and the still-absent light, weather, smelting, farming, and redstone families. I land at 37 with ties broken downward: the world now has destinations and inhabitants, but caves still render daylit, no mountain or river has ever been witnessed, and structures are flavor-grade rather than village-grade.

Banks weight-2 smelting and legitimizes the iron tool tier.

top_gaps.title: Farming: seeds, tilled soil, wheat growth, bread
top_gaps.detail: Lowest-risk untouched family: grass tufts (id 14) drop seeds, right-click dirt/grass with a new hoe (or any tool) makes tilled soil, wheat as a cross-rendered block (the cr: path already renders flowers/tufts) with 2-3 gt-driven growth stages, harvest -> wheat -> bread recipe into the existing FOOD table. Banks weight-2 farming and deepens the food loop beyond apples/porkchops.

top_gaps.title: Real light propagation + torches + light-linked spawning
top_gaps.detail: The biggest single unlock and the heart of the 65-band: per-block skylight/blocklight flood fill folded into buildChunk's vertex-color pass, torch item/block with emissive falloff, genuinely dark caves, and hostile spawns gated on computed light level instead of gDL (banks weight-3 light engine + weight-1 light-linked spawning + the scorecard emissive-blocks wow). High risk: touches every remesh, and a perf regression in the rAF-starved harness would contaminate the whole panel's evidence.

top_gaps.title: Weather: rain/snow with sky integration
top_gaps.detail: Seeded rain events on the gt cycle: stretched particle quads above the player (extend the existing 320-cap particle system), fog/sky dimming during rain via the updSky hooks already in place, snow instead of rain in the snowy biome, and a CI(x,z).h heightmap check so precipitation stops indoors/under overhangs - skipping occlusion would read half-working and fail harsh. Banks weight-2 weather.

top_gaps.title: Combat depth: swords, attack damage table, zombie drops
top_gaps.detail: Round the verified combat loop to full family weight: sword recipes per tier (the pickT texture generator generalizes), an attack-damage table replacing the binary PICKT?3:2 at index.html:720, zombie death drop (rotten flesh as risky FOOD), and verify the tool-bonus branch that has never run live. Cheap because attackMob/drops/FOOD substrates all exist.

top_gaps.title: Villages: multi-hut clusters on the structure system
top_gaps.detail: Upgrade the proven micro-structure machinery (index.html:295-308) from single-chunk ruins to rarer 2-4 hut clusters: plank walls, log corners, door gaps, a gravel path, torch posts once lighting exists; optionally a villager reskin on the mob substrate wandering between huts. Moves the weight-3 family from ~1 to ~2+ credited. Cross-chunk placement is the risk - the current candidate-at-(8,8) trick avoids it and a naive expansion breaks chunk-border determinism.

top_gaps.title: Redstone-like logic v0: lever, wire, lamp/door
top_gaps.detail: Weight-4 family at zero, but defer behind the light engine (a lamp without emissive light reads as a stub): lever block toggling a powered flag, wire blocks flood-filling power like the light BFS, a door block that opens and a lamp that lights. Even a minimal verified loop banks ~1.5; attempting it before light propagation halves its value and doubles its risk.

panel.summary: Round 4 is the band-50 round through my lens: the weight-4 crafting family converted from the run's only probe-proven fail to a fully live-verified chain (log to planks to sticks to pick, real mouse events, exact consumption, leftovers, splitting), and the mob family is demonstrably real rather than a stub - pig and zombie share a genuine entity substrate with world collision, AI states, bidirectional combat, hurt-flash, knockback, typed drops, and a verified death-by-pack respawn loop. I place the whole build at 40, a +5 delta over the merged 35: roughly +1.5 crafting, +3.2 from the two mob families (discounted because natural spawn/despawn/sun-burn were unobservable under this round's tiny frame pumps and the player-knockback fail is verified), +0.9 from live-verified micro-structures (ruins with crafting tables, desert wells), and +0.4 from input-cluster and conversion spillover. The survival loop the 50-anchor describes - inventory, crafting, health/damage, basic mobs - is now substantively satisfied; what caps the score is the still-imperfect sub-43 checklist plus 18 of 40 family weights provably unbanked: smelting, farming, light propagation, weather, and redstone are grep-proven absent, light-linked spawning has no light to link to, and villages are only embryonic. Next round's highest-leverage order is the cheap ecology-evidence + knockback lane first (converts withheld credit), then smelting and farming as low-risk named families, with the light engine as the big high-risk swing that unlocks two families plus cave atmosphere at once.

panel.confidence: Medium-high. The stability floor is the best-evidenced part of the build: zero console errors and zero __vox.errors across a session including two loads, a player death, 8 simultaneous mobs with particle bursts, and the full crafting choreography; boot gate PASS; reload artifacts conclusively exonerated as harness noise. The mob layer — my round-3 'highest risk to the zero-error streak' warning — landed with visible allocation discipline (module-level scratch vars, shared geometries, material-swap caching, MOBMAX 8 + despawn radius) and no observed regression. Residual swing ~±1.5: FPS was unmeasurable live this round (visibility throttling; main-loop evidence asserts 120 when rendering, 60/0 readings are artifacts), natural spawn ticks / light-linked gating / zombie sunburn could not be observed in <1s frame pumps, and the panel's pricing of micro-structures against the weight-3 villages family is a judgment call.

Carried round-3 bit-identical freeze proof; updMobs and updDrops are inside the same `active&&!dead` gate (index.html:998), so the new entities inherit the proven freeze semantics.

Session was continuous hidden-resume cycles with even smaller frame pumps than round 3 (~0.05-0.15s); player AND mobs resumed cleanly every time — probe confirmed mobs kept y exactly at ground level across pumps, never sank or embedded; dt clamp .05 + 0.9-block vertical substepping now applied to both player and mob paths.
Teleports to structures at (264,28,424) and (104,30,264) and the floater-scan region around (560,115) all meshed and screenshot clean; 7ms/frame budget queue unchanged.
56x61-block floater scan found 0 non-foliage anomalies; structure rings and well screenshots show no seams; carried multi-region evidence on an unchanged mesher.

Save/load round-trip re-verified this round: dug block still air after reload, inventory slots identical, dusk clock persisted; chunks memoization + edits-diff re-apply (editB at genChunk) proven again in play.
Carried ±800 coverage plus this round's structure hunts across a 600-block radius (findStructures(300) -> 5 sites, two visited and block-verified).

Drops: 64-cap with shift-eviction + 60s TTL + per-id cached geometries (index.html:644-660); mobs: MOBMAX 8, despawn at pd>56, shared geometries/materials with deliberate no-dispose on rmMob (line 701), hurt-flash swaps a shared material reference cached in userData; probe ran 8 mobs + bursts with no sign of degradation.

dt=min(dt,.05) in loop, mdt clamp in mineTick, vel.y clamp -45, vertical substepping for player (line 515) and mobs (line 764); an entire probe session of irregular ~0.05-0.15s pumps produced zero physics anomalies.

Right-click half-stack splitting verified live (8-plank stack -> cursor 4 / slot 4); exact decrement arithmetic confirmed (planks 9, logs 9, sticks 2 after the choreography); UI rebuilt only on open, memory-bounded.

item_verdicts.evidence: Zombie night chase (position series converging on player, yaw facing), melee for exactly 2/hit, 8-mob pack death and respawn loop all probe-verified; mob-side knockback works (vx/vz=6, vy=4, stun); docked inside the family for the player-knockback fail and unverified sunburn.

item_verdicts.evidence: Probe verified_fail: zombie hit left player position and velocity bit-identical (vel {0,0,0}); damage() applies hp loss + flash + thump only — no impulse path exists in the function (index.html:980-982).

Vertex colors remain face-brightness x AO only; cave interiors render daylit; no emissive block exists in the 1061-line file.
All absent from the file — no furnace, crop, rain, or logic block code paths exist; confirmed by full code read.

top_gaps.title: Deterministic sim-advance test hook (__vox.tick)
top_gaps.detail: The probe environment can no longer sustain >1s of continuous sim (~0.05-0.15s per pump), which left natural spawn waves, the gDL light-linked gate, zombie sunburn, and the 0.8s drop-pickup window all unverifiable — real banked weight is being left on the table every round. Add a __vox.tick(seconds) hook that steps physics/updMobs/updDrops/gt in fixed 16ms slices synchronously inside one eval (idempotent, no background pumping — that wedged the renderer in round 3). Converts the weight-1 light-linked family plus the unverified mob clauses for zero gameplay-code risk.

top_gaps.title: Player knockback impulse on zombie hit
top_gaps.detail: The round's only verified fail: damage() (index.html:980) applies hp/flash/thump with no impulse, while mobs hit by the player DO get vx/vz=6,vy=4. Pass an optional attacker position into damage() and apply a scaled impulse to vel — the physics integration (clamps, substepping) already handles it safely. Small, self-contained, completes the hostile-mob family's combat-with-stakes story.

top_gaps.title: Chunk-data eviction using the proven edits diff
top_gaps.detail: chunks Map (index.html:255) still grows ~16KB per visited chunk forever — carried unaddressed for a third round, and mob spawn checks now call getB on far columns too. The blocker was solved in round 2: editB already reconstructs player edits per chunk at genChunk (line 309), and this round's save/load probe re-proved regeneration+diff correctness. Evict pristine far Uint8Arrays alongside dropMesh's radius logic.

top_gaps.title: Frame-guard granularity and recovery
top_gaps.detail: loop()'s single try/catch (index.html:1007) warns once then swallows forever, and the protected body now contains five subsystems (physics, particles, mining, drops, mobs) plus renderer.render — a persistent throw in updMobs silently freezes sim AND render with one console line. Split sim/render guards, reset frameErr on a timer, and push frame errors into __vox.errors so probes see them. Carried from round 3; the surface it protects doubled since.

top_gaps.title: CCmap scoped eviction instead of cliff clear()
top_gaps.detail: CI() still nukes all 400k cached column structs at the threshold (index.html:249) — tens of MB resident, then a multi-octave fbm recompute storm across every loaded chunk. The cache is now hit by the per-second mob spawner, HUD, structure scans, and genChunk's 484-call tree pass, so both the ceiling and the cliff grew this round. Key entries by chunk and evict with dropMesh radius.

top_gaps.title: Per-type AI function table before mob #3
top_gaps.detail: updMobs (index.html:727-770) inlines zombie/pig behavior as if-else branches inside the shared movement/collision loop. Fine at 2 types; a third (skeleton, creeper, sheep) starts multiplying branch surface inside the hot per-frame loop. Extract MOBDEF[type].ai(M,dt) returning desired wScx/wScz — pure refactor, zero behavior change, keeps the otherwise excellent entity substrate (shared entColl, caps, scratch vars) extensible.

top_gaps.title: Pooled remesh scratch buffers
top_gaps.detail: buildChunk (index.html:391-445) still allocates fresh JS arrays and new GPU buffers per remesh, up to 3 chunks per edit — carried from round 2, verified non-biting at 120 FPS, but farming/smelting/light lanes will multiply rebuild frequency. Reuse module-level typed-array scratch with a high-water mark before GC bursts surface as hitching.

top_gaps.title: Autosave cost and quota visibility
top_gaps.detail: save() (index.html:469) JSON.stringifies the entire edits Map + inventory every 15s on the main thread, and quota failures are silently swallowed — invisible data loss on long sessions. Debounce on edit-count delta and surface one HUD warning when setItem throws. Carried from round 3, unchanged.

panel.summary: Through the perf/stability lens this round is the strongest delta yet: my round-3 'highest risk' warning — that entity AI would break the zero-error/120-FPS streak — did not materialize, because the mob layer landed as the best-engineered subsystem in the file (MOBMAX 8 + despawn radius, shared geometries/materials with no-dispose churn, module-level scratch vars, material-swap caching, the same clamp+substep physics discipline as the player), and the session banked zero console errors through 8 simultaneous zombies, a player death, and the full crafting choreography. The round-3 load-bearing crafting fail is fixed and probe-proven end-to-end, flipping the weight-4 family and closing the survival loop (mine -> craft -> hunt -> eat -> night threat -> death -> respawn) in verified play, which with the pig/zombie/structures evidence moves my estimate from 35 to 38 — held down from 39 by the player-knockback fail, the unverifiable spawn-tick/light-linked clauses, and harsh ties-down. The debit side of my domain is stagnation: all five carried engineering gaps (unbounded chunks Map, CCmap cliff-clear, warn-once frame guard, unpooled remeshes, autosave serialization) went unaddressed for another round, so the feature surface is growing faster than the foundations under it, and the single try/catch now silently protects five subsystems. The most valuable next-round perf-lane spend is a deterministic __vox.tick hook — the probe environment's shrinking frame pumps are now the binding constraint on converting real, already-coded behavior into bankable evidence.

lanes.name: lane-1-knockback-and-evidence-hooks
lanes.goal: Fix the round's only probe-verified fail (no player knockback) and add the deterministic sim/audio test hooks that convert ~2.5 MPS of already-coded-but-unobservable behavior: natural mob spawning, zombie sun-burn, distance despawn, drop auto-collect, and audio audibility. All five judges flagged the shrinking frame pumps as the binding constraint on banking real credit; this lane removes it permanently. Est. +1.7 MPS, low risk.
lanes.spec:
All edits in game/index.html. Touches: damage()/physics() impulse path, a simStep extraction from loop(), spawnTick extraction from updMobs, and the __vox TEST API. Do NOT touch the mesher, worldgen (CI/genChunk/rawH), textures, or the inventory/crafting UI.

(1) PLAYER KNOCKBACK. The player's horizontal vel is overwritten from input every frame (physics(), line 503: vel.x=(-sn*f+cs*s)*sp), so a one-shot impulse would be erased next frame — add a decaying impulse channel instead. Next to the player state declarations (~line 452) add: let kbx=0,kbz=0;. In physics(), immediately after the vel.x/vel.z assignment at line 503, add: vel.x+=kbx;vel.z+=kbz;const kdec=Math.min(1,dt*5);kbx-=kbx*kdec;kbz-=kbz*kdec;. Change damage(n) (line 980) to damage(n,sx,sz): after the hp deduction, if sx!==undefined { const kl=Math.hypot(pos.x-sx,pos.z-sz)||1; kbx=(pos.x-sx)/kl*7; kbz=(pos.z-sz)/kl*7; if(!fly)vel.y=Math.max(vel.y,3.4); }. Update ONLY the zombie melee call site (line 751) to damage(2,M.x,M.z); fall/drown/starve/cactus calls stay 1-arg (no impulse — intentional). In the respawn handler (line 985) add kbx=kbz=0;.

(2) simStep EXTRACTION + __vox.step(seconds). Extract the active-branch body of loop() (line 998: gt+=dt;physics(dt);updParts(dt);mineTick(now);updDrops(dt);updMobs(dt);) into function simStep(dt,now){...} and have loop() call simStep(dt,now) under the same active&&!dead guard — bit-identical behavior, the pause/freeze gate (line 997) is untouched. Add to __vox: step:(secs)=>{secs=Math.max(0,Math.min(30,+secs||0));const t0=performance.now();const fx=1/60;let t=0,i=0;while(t<secs&&i<1800){if(dead)break;if(i%15===0)updSky();simStep(fx,t0+t*1000);t+=fx;i++;}updSky();return {t:+t.toFixed(3),mobs:mobs.length,hp,dead};}. Synchronous, idempotent, returns when done — no background pumping (the round-3 zombie-eval trap). Passing synthetic now values is safe: mineTick computes mdt from now-mtLast and clamps to .05, and nextPlace compares against the same monotonic series. The periodic updSky() keeps gDL current so sun-burn (line 746, gDL>.5 for 1.5s) actually fires during stepped time.

(3) spawnTick EXTRACTION + __vox.tickSpawns(n). Extract the body of the if(mobSpawnT>=1) block in updMobs (lines 731-740, WITHOUT the !TEST||spawnOn gate) into function spawnTick(){...}; updMobs keeps the timer and gate and calls spawnTick(). Add to __vox: tickSpawns:(n)=>{updSky();for(let i=0;i<Math.min(60,n||1);i++)spawnTick();return mobs.map(M=>({type:M.type,x:+M.x.toFixed(1),z:+M.z.toFixed(1)}));}. Keep the per-candidate validation (ci.h<=SEA reject, SOLID-above reject, ci.tp===1 for pigs, counts/caps) exactly as-is so lane 3 can later wrap it.

(4) AUDIO AUDIBILITY PROBE. Add to __vox: audioCheck:async()=>{...} — if typeof OfflineAudioContext==='undefined' return null. For each of [sBreak,sPlace,chirp]: create a fresh OfflineAudioContext(1,22050,44100); save oldAC=AC,oldM=master,oldN=nbuf; point AC at the offline ctx, create master=oac.createGain() (gain .7, connect to destination), build nbuf as a 2s noise buffer on the offline ctx (same fill as audioInit line 776); call the generator; RESTORE the three globals immediately (before awaiting); const buf=await oac.startRendering(); scan channel 0 for peak |amplitude|. Return {break:pk1,place:pk2,chirp:pk3} with 4-decimal peaks. Wrap the global swap in try/finally so a throw can never leave AC pointing at a dead offline context.

Constraints: zero new per-frame allocations in physics/updMobs hot paths (kbx/kbz are module-level scalars); no change to spawn-search determinism (lines 473-477); ?test=1 must still boot with spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) on seed 1337.
lanes.acceptance: All via ?test=1 + __vox, in order: (a) Boot gate: fresh load, getState() spawn exactly x=116.5, y≈29.02, z=115.5, errors []. (b) Knockback: setTime(0); spawnMob('zombie', px+0.8, undefined, pz); record p0=getState(); step(1.5); assert hp < 20 AND horizontal displacement from p0 > 0.5 directed away from the zombie AND a vel.y pop was observable (sample() during step or y rose above start); then with no zombies, step(2) and assert walk speed normal (impulse fully decayed, |vel|≈input-driven). (c) Fall damage still impulse-free: fly up 10, drop, hp loss with no horizontal displacement. (d) Natural spawns: clearMobs(); setTime(0); tickSpawns(5) → 1-4 zombies, every one at horizontal distance 18-32 from player, each standing on a solid non-water column (block(x,h,z) solid, colInfo h>SEA); setTime(12); clearMobs(); teleport to plains (tp===1); tickSpawns(5) → only pigs, ≤4. (e) Sun-burn: spawnMob('zombie') at noon, step(2) → mobs() empty and a particle burst occurred (screenshot or parts spike). (f) Despawn: spawnMob, setCam 60+ blocks away, step(1.1) → mobs() empty. (g) Drop auto-collect: brk() a grass block, step(1.2) → dropList() empty and inventory count incremented (the 0.8s immunity then magnet+pickup completed). (h) audioCheck() → all three peaks > 0.01. (i) Regressions: pause() then step(0) untouched/gt frozen via skyInfo; save/load round-trip still passes; __vox.errors and console both empty after the whole script; FPS unchanged when rendering.

lanes.name: lane-2-farming-family
lanes.goal: Bank the weight-2 farming family (grep-proven absent for four rounds): seeds from grass tufts, till-and-plant on dirt/grass, 3-stage wheat growth, harvest, and bread into the existing FOOD pipeline. Also performs the one-time texture-atlas expansion (the atlas is 31/32 tiles full) that lane 3 reuses. Lowest-risk untouched family per the systems judge. Est. +1.3 MPS, low risk.
lanes.spec:
All edits in game/index.html. Touches: texture atlas constants, block/item data tables, doBreak/tryPlace farming branches, a new updFarm ticker, boot rebuild, __vox hooks. Do NOT touch genChunk/CI/rawH (no natural crops — determinism stays bit-identical), the mesher, mobs, damage, or audio.

(1) ATLAS EXPANSION (one-time, shared with lane 3). Line 131: TC.height=64 → 128. Line 373 tuv(): second component divisor /4 → /8 (1-((t>>3)+.02+(1-v)*.96)/8). Lines 176-178 avg block: getImageData(0,0,128,64) → (0,0,128,128) and loop t<32 → t<64. Everything else (T(), drawIcon, setCrackTile, dropGeo) already derives from t&7 / t>>3 and keeps working; crack tiles 21-24 and all existing tile rows are unmoved. Verify visually after this step before continuing.

(2) NEW TILES (32-38) via T(): 32 farmland top (dirt base ~140,100,68 with darker furrow rows every 4px, like tile 19's banding but brown); 33 wheat sprout (sparse short green shoots from bottom, transparent elsewhere — use alpha like tile 16's style); 34 wheat mid (taller green-yellow stalks); 35 wheat mature (full-height golden stalks ~200,170,60 with seed heads); 36 seeds item icon (scatter of 6-8 small pale dots); 37 wheat item icon (tied golden sheaf); 38 bread icon (rounded loaf ~170,120,60 with lighter top). Transparent = simply don't P() the pixel (canvas starts clear; matO alphaTest .4 and cr-quads handle it, same as flowers).

(3) DATA REGISTRATION. BL (line 186): 18:{t:[32,2,2]} farmland (dirt sides/bottom), 19:{cr:33},20:{cr:34},21:{cr:35} wheat stages. Replace SOLID (line 189) with: const NSOLID={5:1,13:1,14:1,19:1,20:1,21:1};const SOLID=b=>b>0&&!NSOLID[b]; (OPQ alias unchanged — lane 3 will append its torch id here). NAMES: 18:'Farmland',19:'Wheat',20:'Wheat',21:'Wheat',107:'Seeds',108:'Wheat',109:'Bread'. DROPS: 18:2 (dirt), 19:107, 20:107, 21:108. HARD: 18:.45, 19:.05, 20:.05, 21:.05. ITILE: 107:36,108:37,109:38. FOOD (line 971): add 109:5. RECIPES (line 213): add {in:{108:3},out:109,n:1}.

(4) SEED SOURCES. In doBreak (line 594 area), after the apple special-case add: if(h.b===14&&h3f(h.x,h.y,h.z)<.4)spawnDrop(h.x,h.y,h.z,107); (deterministic ~40% of grass tufts — position-hashed so the probe can pre-compute which tufts pay out). Mature wheat bonus: if(h.b===21)spawnDrop(h.x,h.y,h.z,107); (alongside the DROPS[21]=108 wheat).

(5) TILL+PLANT. In tryPlace (line 604), insert BEFORE the it.id>=100 early-return at line 607: if(it&&it.id===107){const h2=ray();if(h2&&h2.n[1]===1&&(h2.b===1||h2.b===2||h2.b===18)&&getB(h2.x,h2.y+1,h2.z)===0){if(h2.b!==18)setB(h2.x,h2.y,h2.z,18);setB(h2.x,h2.y+1,h2.z,19);farm.set(h2.x+','+(h2.y+1)+','+h2.z,gt+20+h3f(h2.x,h2.y,h2.z)*20);it.count--;if(it.count<=0)inv[slot]=null;touched(h2.x,h2.y,h2.z);updCounts();sPlace();}return;} Flag in a code comment: pragmatic substitution — seeds till on use, no hoe item (state it in the file header too).

(6) GROWTH. Module-level: const farm=new Map();let farmT=0;. function updFarm(dt){farmT+=dt;if(farmT<1)return;farmT=0;for(const[k,due]of farm){if(gt<due)continue;const p=k.split(','),x=+p[0],y=+p[1],z=+p[2],b=getB(x,y,z);if(b===19||b===20){setB(x,y,z,b+1);touched(x,y,z);farm.set(k,gt+20+h3f(x,y+b,z)*20);}else farm.delete(k);}} Call updFarm(dt) by appending it inside simStep (lane 1 created it; if absent, append in the loop active branch at line 998). Crop-pop cascade: extend the support-removal loop in doBreak (line 597, ab===12||ab===13||ab===14) to also match 19,20,21 so breaking farmland/dirt pops the crop above (no drop, same as flowers — note it).

(7) BOOT REBUILD. After the edits Map is populated from SV (line 464), add: for(const[ek,ev]of edits)if(ev===19||ev===20)farm.set(ek,gt+15); (mature 21 needs no timer) — crops persist and resume growing across save/load.

(8) __vox HOOKS. growCrops:()=>{for(const k of farm.keys())farm.set(k,gt-1);farmT=1;updFarm(1);return [...farm.keys()].length;}, farmInfo:()=>[...farm.entries()].map(([k,d])=>({k,due:+d.toFixed(1)})).

Constraints: farm Map is bounded by player planting (no natural generation); updFarm is throttled to 1Hz; no per-frame allocations beyond the existing iterator. Seeds are id 107 ≥100 so the generic block-place path can never place them as a block; the ghost preview's id<100 gate already excludes them.
lanes.acceptance: Via ?test=1 + __vox: (a) Atlas regression FIRST: screenshots of the spawn beach vista, a stone wall, the hotbar with all 9 starter icons, and a mid-mine crack overlay — all visually identical in character to round 4 (UV divisor change is the highest-risk edit; verify before anything else). (b) Seeds: locate a grass tuft (block id 14) whose h3f(x,y,z)<.4 by scanning blocks near a plains teleport, brk() it → dropList contains id 107. (c) Plant: give(107,4); aim at the top face of a grass block with air above; place() → block(x,y,z)===18 and block(x,y+1,z)===19, seed count decremented, 'place' sfx logged. (d) Growth: growCrops() → block becomes 20; growCrops() again → 21; farmInfo() drops the entry after maturity. (e) Harvest: brk() the mature wheat → drops include one id 108 AND one id 107. (f) Bread: give(108,3); craft(109) returns true; eat() with bread selected → hunger rises exactly +5, count decremented, 'eat' sfx logged. (g) Cascade: plant on dirt, brk() the farmland block → crop above is gone (block(x,y+1,z)===0), no floating cross. (h) Persistence: plant a stage-19 crop, __vox.save(), reload ?test=1&load=1 → crop block persists and growCrops() still advances it. (i) Regressions: fresh ?test=1 spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5); findStructures(300) returns the same 2 ruins + 3 wells; full crafting choreography (log→planks→sticks→pick) still passes; zero console errors, __vox.errors empty.

lanes.name: lane-3-cave-light-and-torches
lanes.goal: Seed the weight-3 light-propagation family — the build's biggest 43+ ceiling, flagged by all five judges: depth-based skylight so caves finally render dark, a craftable/placeable torch with a local light pool, torch-suppressed zombie spawning (converts the weight-1 light-linked family leg), and a __vox.lightAt evidence hook. Isolated as its own lane because it touches buildChunk and every remesh path — the verified zero-seam/AO record is at stake. Est. +2.5 MPS, high risk, sequenced last.
lanes.spec:
All edits in game/index.html. Runs after lanes 1-2 (relies on lane 1's spawnTick extraction and lane 2's expanded atlas). Touches: one new tile, torch block registration, a torch registry, buildChunk vertex-color math, setB/doBreak torch handling, dropGeo cr-fix, one light gate inside spawnTick, __vox hooks. Do NOT touch CI/genChunk/rawH terrain logic (spawn determinism), the player physics, the inventory UI, or audio.

(1) TORCH CONTENT. Tile 39 via T(): vertical 2px-wide stick (~124,84,46) rising from bottom-center to y≈5, topped with a 3x3 glowing head (255,220,90 core, 255,160,40 fringe), transparent elsewhere. BL[22]={cr:39}; add 22:1 to lane 2's NSOLID set (non-solid, non-opaque); NAMES 22:'Torch'; HARD 22:.05; DROPS 22:22; RECIPES add {in:{100:1,10:1},out:22,n:4} (stick + coal-ore → 4 torches; comment the ore-not-coal-item pragmatic substitution, same as the iron pick at line 212). CRITICAL dropGeo FIX (line 647): tile selection currently reads bl.t[...] which is undefined for cr-blocks and would throw the moment a torch drop spawns — change to: const tile=id>=100?ITILE[id]:(bl.cr!==undefined?bl.cr:bl.t[f===2?0:f===3?1:2]);.

(2) TORCH REGISTRY. const torchR=new Map(); // chunkKey 'cx,cz' -> Map(localIdx -> [wx,wy,wz]). In setB (line 316), before the write read const old=d[i]; after the write: if(old===22||v===22){const tk=(x>>4)+','+(z>>4);let s=torchR.get(tk);if(v===22){if(!s){s=new Map();torchR.set(tk,s);}s.set(i,[x,y,z]);}else if(s){s.delete(i);if(!s.size)torchR.delete(tk);}}. Boot rebuild: after the edits Map loads (line 464), for(const[ek,ev]of edits)if(ev===22){parse x,y,z from ek and register the same way}. No genChunk hook needed — torches are never naturally generated.

(3) MESHER LIGHT (the high-risk core — keep it exactly this shape). At the top of buildChunk (line 391), precompute once per build: (a) const hcol=new Int16Array(18*18); for lx -1..16, lz -1..16: hcol[(lx+1)*18+(lz+1)]=CI(wx0+lx,wz0+lz).h; (b) const tlist=[]; for the 9 chunk keys (cx-1..cx+1)×(cz-1..cz+1), push every torchR entry's [wx,wy,wz]. Helper inside buildChunk: function lightOf(lx,ly,lz){const d2=hcol[(lx+1)*18+(lz+1)]-ly;let L=d2<=0?1:Math.max(.1,1-d2*.16);for(let q=0;q<tlist.length;q++){const T2=tlist[q],dx=wx0+lx+.5-T2[0]-.5,dy=ly+.5-T2[1]-.5,dz=lz+.5-T2[2]-.5;const dist=Math.sqrt(dx*dx+dy*dy+dz*dz);if(dist<7){const t3=1-dist/7;if(t3>L)L=t3;}}return L;} — pure local-array reads, zero string keys, zero allocation in the face loop. Solid faces: at line 427 change const li=F.l*AOT[a]; to const li=F.l*AOT[a]*lightOf(qx,qy,qz); (qx/qy/qz are the face-adjacent cell, already in scope; compute lightOf ONCE per face before the 4-vertex loop, not per vertex). Cross-rendered blocks (line 402): multiply the .95 by (b===22?1:lightOf(x,y,z)) — torches render self-lit. Water quads: untouched. AO indices, seam-handling, and the index-flip heuristic (line 431) must not change.

(4) TORCH REMESH RADIUS. A torch's 7-block pool can cross into neighbors that touched() won't rebuild. In doBreak and tryPlace's setB call sites — or cleanest, right after touched(x,y,z) wherever the placed/broken id is 22 — add: for(let cx2=(x-7)>>4;cx2<=(x+7)>>4;cx2++)for(let cz2=(z-7)>>4;cz2<=(z+7)>>4;cz2++)remesh(cx2,cz2); (≤4 chunks, within the proven per-edit rebuild budget).

(5) LIGHT-GATED SPAWNING. Module helper: function lightAtW(x,y,z){const d2=CI(Math.floor(x),Math.floor(z)).h-y;const sky=d2<=0?1:Math.max(.1,1-d2*.16);let tl=0;const ck=(Math.floor(x)>>4)+','+(Math.floor(z)>>4); /* check the 3x3 chunk neighborhood of torchR around x,z */ for each registered torch within dist<7: tl=max(tl,1-dist/7); return {sky,torch:tl,eff:Math.max(sky*gDL,tl)};}. Inside lane 1's spawnTick, in the zombie branch only, after the existing column checks (line 738) add: if(lightAtW(sx+.5,ci.h+1,sz+.5).eff>=.15)continue; — at an unlit surface column eff=gDL<.12 (night) so current behavior is preserved bit-for-bit; a torch-lit column is rejected. Pig branch untouched. Also extract the per-candidate validation into function trySpawnAt(type,sx,sz) returning 'ok'|'water'|'solid'|'light'|'biome'|'cap' and have spawnTick's random candidates call it, so the gate is probe-addressable.

(6) __vox HOOKS. lightAt:(x,y,z)=>lightAtW(x,y,z), spawnAt:(type,x,z)=>{updSky();return trySpawnAt(type,Math.floor(x),Math.floor(z));}, torches:()=>{const o=[];for(const s of torchR.values())for(const t of s.values())o.push(t);return o;}.

Constraints: lightOf must be allocation-free and array-backed (the mesher rebuilds up to 3 chunks per edit at 120fps — the perf judge's streak is the panel's evidence substrate); hcol covers exactly the -1..16 neighbor range the face loop can reference; no change to chunk data, CI, or spawn search means seed-1337 spawn and findStructures stay bit-identical.
lanes.acceptance: Via ?test=1 + __vox: (a) Boot + determinism FIRST: fresh load spawns exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5); findStructures(300) unchanged; zero errors. (b) Skylight: at the spawn surface lightAt(x, h+1, z).sky === 1; dig or teleport into cave air ≥6 below the heightmap (verify depth via colInfo h minus y) → lightAt(...).sky ≤ 0.2; screenshot the cave interior vs a surface shot — interior visibly darker (the first dark cave in five rounds). (c) Torch: give(100,1) + give(10,1); craft(22) returns true yielding 4 torches with icon+name in hotbar; place one in the dark cave → block()===22, lightAt at distance 2 reports torch ≥ 0.7 and at distance 5 ≥ 0.25; screenshot shows a bright pool falling off into darkness. (d) Break the torch → lightAt torch component returns 0, remesh clean, torch drop spawns and is collectible (this exercises the dropGeo cr-fix — it must not throw). (e) Cross-chunk: place a torch ≤7 blocks from a chunk border, verify the neighbor chunk's adjacent faces brightened (screenshot, no light seam at the border). (f) Spawn gate: setTime(0); spawnAt('zombie', x, z) at a torch-lit column (eff≥.15) returns 'light'; the same call at an unlit valid column returns 'ok' and adds a mob; setTime(0) + tickSpawns(5) with no torches behaves exactly as lane 1's baseline (1-4 zombies at r18-32). (g) Persistence: place a torch, save(), reload ?test=1&load=1 → torches() still lists it and lightAt still reports its pool (boot registry rebuild). (h) Regression sweep, mandatory: re-shoot the desert 56x61 scan region and both structure sites for seams/holes; one concave-corner screenshot confirming AO survives the light multiply; 170-op rapid place/break burst with zero errors and no hitching; getState().fps sane while rendering; pause() still freezes; the full crafting choreography still passes.

built.changes_summary: All edits in game/index.html. (1) Player knockback: added module-level `let kbx=0,kbz=0;` next to player state (~line 453); in physics(), right after the input vel.x/vel.z overwrite, added `vel.x+=kbx;vel.z+=kbz;` plus exponential decay (`kdec=Math.min(1,dt*5)`); damage(n) became damage(n,sx,sz) — when source coords are passed it writes a 7 m/s impulse away from the source and pops vel.y to >=3.4 when not flying; only the zombie melee call site passes coords (`damage(2,M.x,M.z)`), fall/drown/starve/cactus stay 1-arg; respawn handler clears kbx/kbz. (2) Extracted the active-branch body of loop() into `simStep(dt,now)` (bit-identical: gt+=dt;physics;updParts;mineTick;updDrops;updMobs) and loop() now calls it under the same active&&!dead guard; added `__vox.step(secs)` — synchronous fixed 1/60 stepping, 30s/1800-iter cap, dead-break, updSky() every 15 sub-steps + once at end so gDL-driven logic (sun-burn) fires during stepped time. (3) Extracted the spawn block body from updMobs into `spawnTick()` (per-candidate validation unchanged: SEA reject, SOLID-above reject, tp===1 for pigs, counts/caps); updMobs keeps the 1s timer and the !TEST||spawnOn gate; added `__vox.tickSpawns(n)` (updSky first, <=60 ticks, returns mob type/x/z list). (4) Added `__vox.audioCheck()` — per generator [sBreak,sPlace,chirp] builds a fresh OfflineAudioContext(1,22050,44100), swaps AC/master/nbuf globals inside try/finally (restored before awaiting startRendering), builds a 2s noise nbuf on the offline ctx, scans channel 0 for peak amplitude, returns {break,place,chirp} at 4 decimals; returns null when OfflineAudioContext is unavailable. Verified live via the running preview server (port 8911, ?test=1): boot spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) with errors []; knockback hp 20->16 with 2.69-block displacement directly away from the zombie and full decay after (vel 0 with no input); fall damage hpLoss 9 with horiz displacement 0.000; night tickSpawns(5) -> 4 zombies at distances 21.2/26.3/30.7/31.4 all on solid dry columns; noon plains tickSpawns(5) -> 4 pigs only; sun-burn zombie removed within step(2) at noon; pig despawned after step(1.1) at 70 blocks; grass brk + step(1.2) -> dropList empty, dirt count 10->11; audioCheck {break:.2814, place:.32, chirp:.0279} all > .01; pause+step(0) left gt and sky hex frozen; __vox.errors 0 and console clean (only the pre-existing three.js r160 classic-script deprecation warning) after the whole script.
built.notes: Static checks: file ends with </html>, inline script extracted and node --check passes. Risks/follow-ups: (1) step() advances mtLast into a synthetic future (t0+t*1000); mineTick clamps mdt to [0,.05] so the first real frame after a step simply sees mdt=0 — harmless, but any future code subtracting from mtLast without the max(,0) clamp would regress. (2) audioCheck logs break/place/chirp entries into __vox.sfx via logS — harmless for current acceptance (only errors are asserted empty) but a strict sfx-sequence assertion written later should account for it. (3) tickSpawns intentionally bypasses the spawnOn gate (it calls spawnTick directly), so a harness that wants gated behavior must keep using setSpawning + step. (4) spawnTick validation block kept verbatim for lane 3 to wrap. (5) FPS reads 60 in the preview tab because the browser vsync-caps rAF; no per-frame allocations were added to physics/updMobs hot paths (kbx/kbz are module scalars), so the headless 120 FPS budget is unaffected.

Deviation 2: on 20→21 updFarm deletes the entry immediately instead of re-arming the timer (matches acceptance d 'farmInfo drops the entry after maturity'; spec code left a dangling entry for one extra cycle). updFarm(dt) appended to simStep (line 1027). built.notes: Risks/follow-ups: (1) Lane 3 should append its torch id to the NSOLID table at line 203 (not redefine SOLID) and draw its tiles at 39+ — rows 4-7 of the 128px atlas are now live. (2) Two flagged deviations from spec letter, both behavior-improving: boot farm-rebuild moved after gt init (TDZ at the spec'd location would have thrown at boot), and mature crops drop their farm entry immediately on the 20→21 transition. (3) growCrops only matures a 19-stage crop to 21 after two calls by design (one stage per call). (4) Water faces still don't render against crop blocks (19-21) — crops can't legally occupy water cells via the till path, so unreachable; noted in case a future lane adds water-adjacent planting. (5) The farm Map self-cleans stale entries on the next 1Hz tick after a crop is broken/popped, so farmInfo() can briefly show an entry for a destroyed crop between ticks — harmless and bounded. fps occasionally reads 0 immediately after synchronous __vox eval bursts (sampling-window artifact, recovers to 60 next frame); pre-existing, not introduced by this lane.

built.changes_summary:
All edits in game/index.html (+38/-13 lines, single file).

(1) CONTENT: Tile 39 torch texture (2px stick rising to y=5, 3x3 glowing head 255,220,90 core / 255,160,40 fringe) added after T(38), before the avg[] computation so burst particles get correct colors. BL[22]={cr:39}; NSOLID +22:1; NAMES 22:'Torch'; DROPS 22:22; HARD 22:.05; RECIPES +{in:{100:1,10:1},out:22,n:4} with the ore-not-coal pragmatic-substitution comment. dropGeo cr-fix applied: tile selection now falls back to bl.cr for cr-blocks (torch is the first cr-block with a self-drop; the old bl.t[...] read would have thrown).

(2) REGISTRY: const torchR Map (chunkKey -> Map(localIdx -> [wx,wy,wz])) declared above setB. setB reads old value, writes, then registers/deregisters id-22 cells. Boot rebuild added inside the existing SV edits loop (registers ev===22 entries alongside the editB push).

(3) MESHER: buildChunk precomputes an 18x18 Int16Array of CI heights (-1..16 range) plus a flat tlist from the 3x3 torchR neighborhood; inner lightOf(lx,ly,lz) is allocation-free (skylight: 1 at/above heightmap, -0.16/block below, 0.1 floor; torch: linear 7-block falloff, max-combine). Solid faces: lf computed once per face, li=F.l*AOT[a]*lf. Cross blocks: .95 multiplied by lightOf (torches self-lit, b===22 exempt). Water quads, AO indices, seam handling, index-flip heuristic untouched. NOTE: corrected an obvious spec typo in lightOf (dz needed wz0+lz, mirroring dx's wx0+lx) — without it torch light would be wrong in every chunk except cz=0.

(4) REMESH RADIUS: remeshTorch(x,z) rebuilds the <=4 chunks within x±7,z±7; doBreak and tryPlace route id-22 edits through it instead of touched() (strict superset, avoids double-building the home chunk).

(5) SPAWN GATE: module-level lightAtW(x,y,z) returning {sky,torch,eff:max(sky*gDL,torch)}; per-candidate validation extracted to trySpawnAt(type,sx,sz) -> 'ok'|'water'|'solid'|'light'|'biome'|'cap' with the zombie-only eff>=.15 light gate; spawnTick now calls it (identical RNG consumption, counts, and caps).

(6) __vox: lightAt, spawnAt (with updSky refresh), torches() added.

RUNTIME-VERIFIED via the live preview (?test=1, fresh save-cleared boots): spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) with 0 errors twice; surface sky===1; 6-below sky=0.1; craft(22)=true yielding 4 with icon+name; placed torch reports .714 at dist 2 / .286 at dist 5; spawnAt='light' at the torch column, 'ok' + real zombie at an unlit column, 'water' over the sea; setTime(0)+tickSpawns(5) baseline intact (4 zombies, r20.6-28.3); break -> drop id 22 spawns WITHOUT throwing, registry empties, torch light component 0, drop collected (count 3->4); save -> reload ?load=1 -> torches() lists (116,28,114) and pool intact (boot rebuild); 60 rapid torch place/break ops straddling the x=127/128 chunk border at 7.1ms/op with 0 errors and clean registry. Page left pristine (save cleared, plain ?test=1).
built.notes: Static: file ends with </html>; extracted main script passes node --check. Risks/follow-ups for the probe: (1) Visual screenshots (cave darkness, torch pool falloff, border seam check, concave-corner AO) still need the panel's eyes — my preview viewport rendered squashed, though terrain/water/grass all rendered normally and numeric light values are exact. (2) Skylight is heightmap-depth based by design: player-dug shafts darken (depth below CI.h) but player-built roofs do NOT create darkness, and breaking surface blocks doesn't brighten caves below — consistent with the spec, worth a sentence in any judge brief. (3) Breaking the block UNDER a torch leaves it floating (the plant-pop loop doesn't include 22) — minecraft-parity gap, deliberate scope hold. (4) Torch edits cost ~7ms (up to 4 chunk rebuilds) — fine at 120fps but a burst of torch ops mid-combat could hitch one frame; remeshTorch could be made async via the pend queue later. (5) Spec's lightOf snippet had dz missing wz0; corrected (flagged in changes_summary). (6) tlist is rebuilt per chunk build from up to 9 map lookups — negligible now, revisit only if torch counts reach hundreds per chunk neighborhood.
